title: Indulgent Mexican Kippers Birria
description: A rich and savory Veracruzan-style Birria dish featuring kippers and a clay-pot-cooked broth.

ingredients.name: Kippers ( smoked haddock )
ingredients.notes: look for firm flesh and a pleasant aroma

ingredients.name: Dried Ancho Chilies
ingredients.notes: soak in hot water for at least 30 minutes

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Sear the kippers for 2-3 minutes on each side, then remove and set aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, cumin, paprika, and coriander to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the soaked Ancho Chilies to the pot, along with their liquid, and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add the chicken broth, kippers, and salt to the pot, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the kippers are cooked through and the broth has thickened.
Stir in the lime juice and season with additional salt if needed.
Serve the Birria in bowls, garnished with chopped onion, cilantro, and a dollop of sour cream or Greek yogurt, if desired.
